tossups.question: To reconstruct a sculpture by this artist, Adolf Furtwängler ("FYOORT-vain-glur") attached a head from the Pelagio Palagi to a body kept in Dresden. An inscription calling a wrestler beautiful appears on the little finger of a statue by this artist. Although this artist likely didn't use the number, Mark Barr referenced this sculptor's name when he symbolized the golden ratio. A Lawrence Alma-Tadema painting depicts this artist presenting a frieze of the Panathenaic ("pan-uh-thuh-NAY-ic") games to his friends. Alan LeQuire recreated a sculpture by this artist in the naos of a William Crawford Smith project in Centennial Park, Nashville. This artist and his patron Pericles appear on the shield of a chryselephantine ("chris-ell-uh-FAN-tin") sculpture by him wearing gold and holding a small Nike. For 10 points, name this Athenian sculptor of the Statue of Zeus at Olympia and of the Athena in the Parthenon.

An inscription calling a wrestler beautiful appears on the little finger of a statue by this artist. Although this artist likely didn't use the number, Mark Barr referenced this sculptor's name when he symbolized the golden ratio. A Lawrence Alma-Tadema painting depicts this artist presenting a frieze of the Panathenaic ("pan-uh-thuh-NAY-ic") games to his friends. Alan LeQuire recreated a sculpture by this artist in the naos of a William Crawford Smith project in Centennial Park, Nashville. This artist and his patron Pericles appear on the shield of a chryselephantine ("chris-ell-uh-FAN-tin") sculpture by him wearing gold and holding a small Nike. For 10 points, name this Athenian sculptor of the Statue of Zeus at Olympia and of the Athena in the Parthenon.

bonuses.leadin: Pliny the Elder wrote that Polykleitos won a contest to sculpt a wounded one of these subjects for the Temple of Artemis, which became a popular origin story for five surviving statue types. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Pliny the Elder wrote that Polykleitos won a contest to sculpt a wounded one of these subjects for the Temple of Artemis, which became a popular origin story for five surviving statue types. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these subjects depicted in the Lansdowne, Sosicles, and Mattei types. The Bassae Frieze depicts a centauromachy and two battles against these subjects.

Though most surviving copies are in marble, the five Amazon statues were supposedly in this metal. Boy With Thorn and the Capitoline She-Wolf are classical sculptures made with this metal using the lost-wax process.
This Athenian came second in the contest to sculpt an Amazon. This sculptor included an Amazonomachy on the shield of his colossal Athena in the Parthenon.
parts_sanitized: Name these subjects depicted in the Lansdowne, Sosicles, and Mattei types. The Bassae Frieze depicts a centauromachy and two battles against these subjects.

Though most surviving copies are in marble, the five Amazon statues were supposedly in this metal. Boy With Thorn and the Capitoline She-Wolf are classical sculptures made with this metal using the lost-wax process.
This Athenian came second in the contest to sculpt an Amazon. This sculptor included an Amazonomachy on the shield of his colossal Athena in the Parthenon.

packet.name: D - Chicago B, McGill A, UBC A, UNC A
L (Stanford A, Imperial B, Rutgers A, Dartmouth A, Duke A, Penn A)

bonuses.leadin: A royal portrait by this artist features a medallion of Raphael’s Madonna della Seggiola. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter of Napoleon I on His Imperial Throne, who also portrayed a woman with an unrealistically long back in La Grande Odalisque.

In the painting, Napoleon holds a scepter that has a statuette of this ruler at the end. This ruler inaugurated an artistic period that produced the illuminated Ebbo Gospels and the Palatine Chapel designed by Odo of Metz.
In Ingres’s painting, Napoleon’s pose is modeled on descriptions of this sculpture. Pausanias claims that this sculpture wore a glass robe and held a figurine of Nike.
answers: Charlemagne [or Charles the Great; or Carolus Magnus; prompt on Charles or Carolus] (The period is the Carolingian Renaissance.)
Statue of Zeus at Olympia [or Olympian Zeus; accept answers indicating Phidias’s sculpture of Zeus]
answers_sanitized: Charlemagne [or Charles the Great; or Carolus Magnus; prompt on Charles or Carolus] (The period is the Carolingian Renaissance.)
Statue of Zeus at Olympia [or Olympian Zeus; accept answers indicating Phidias's sculpture of Zeus]

parts_sanitized: Name this painter of Napoleon I on His Imperial Throne, who also portrayed a woman with an unrealistically long back in La Grande Odalisque.

In the painting, Napoleon holds a scepter that has a statuette of this ruler at the end. This ruler inaugurated an artistic period that produced the illuminated Ebbo Gospels and the Palatine Chapel designed by Odo of Metz.
In Ingres's painting, Napoleon's pose is modeled on descriptions of this sculpture. Pausanias claims that this sculpture wore a glass robe and held a figurine of Nike.

bonuses.leadin: Mark Barr used a Greek symbol similar to this sculptor’s name to represent the golden ratio. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this ancient Greek sculptor who depicted an enthroned deity holding a scepter in the Statue of Zeus at Olympia. The Elgin Marbles were created under the supervision of this sculptor.
Phidias created a giant bronze statue of this goddess with the epithet “Promachos”. Phidias also created a statue of this Greek goddess of wisdom for the Parthenon.
Both the Statue of Zeus at Olympia and the Athena Parthenos were examples of these sculptures. The Palaikastro Kouros is one of these sculptures, which are made with gold and ivory.
answers_sanitized: Athena [accept Athena Parthenos; accept Athena Promachos]
